rows.v: Baguette, Brioche, Focaccia, Ciabatta, Italian Loaf
Baguette: crackling crust, chewy crumb; Brioche: enriched, buttery; Focaccia: olive oil, dimpled; Ciabatta: open crumb, airy
Baguette → Champagne, Chablis; Brioche → Sauternes, rosé; Focaccia → Vermentino, Barbera; Ciabatta → Soave, Pinot Grigio
Classic Sourdough, San Francisco Sourdough, Sourdough Rye, Vollkornbrot
Wild yeast + lactic acid bacteria; long cold ferment develops tang, chew, and complex crust
Aged white Burgundy, natural wines, Grüner Veltliner, aged cheddar and charcuterie
Whole Wheat, Multigrain, Sprouted Grain, Pumpernickel, Rye Bread
Nutty, earthy, dense; pumpernickel: darkly roasted, molasses-sweet; rye: tangy and dense
Smoked fish, aged Gouda, Grüner Veltliner, Riesling — the classic Scandinavian smørrebrød pairings
Challah, Hokkaido Milk Bread, Hawaiian Bread, Babka, White Bread, Potato Bread
Soft, pillowy, slightly sweet; enriched with eggs, butter, milk or oil; long shelf life
Challah → Riesling or sparkling; Milk Bread → Champagne; Hawaiian → off-dry Gewurztraminer

groups.name: Middle East & South Asia

rows.v: Pita, Lavash, Naan, Paratha, Roti/Chapati, Dosa
Pita: pocket-forming steam leavening; Naan: tandoor-charred, buttery; Dosa: fermented rice-lentil, crispy
Pita/Lavash → rosé, Albariño; Naan/Paratha → Gewurztraminer, light red; Dosa → Riesling, sparkling

groups.name_es: Oriente Medio y Asia del Sur

rows.v: Cornbread, Tortilla, Arepa, Banana Bread, Beer Bread, Irish Soda Bread, Scone
Leavened with baking soda or powder (no yeast); quick to make; often enriched or sweetened
Cornbread → Viognier, Chardonnay; Beer Bread → craft IPA or the beer used to make it; Scone → Champagne, clotted cream
English Muffin, Crumpet, Bagel, Bao Bun, Beignet, Fry Bread, Youtiao
Diverse textures: nook-heavy (muffin/crumpet), dense-chewy (bagel), pillowy-steamed (bao), airy-fried (beignet)
Bagel + lox → Champagne or Chablis; Beignet → Café au lait or Sauternes; Bao → Riesling or light Pinot Noir
